response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with deep web, mobile, and backend experience, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware requiring SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and deep neural network optimization. The resume shows essentially none of these hardware-architecture competencies, no relevant advanced degree, and no demonstrated low-level performance work on AI accelerators. The mismatch between the candidate's profile and the role's core technical requirements is severe.
strengths: Long, broad software engineering track record across many domains
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and DLang microservices
Strong testing, TDD, and engineering-craft discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip-architecture experience
No demonstrated deep-learning workload optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD background
Educational path (BS only) does not meet the stated PhD/MS/BS+experience profile for this specialized hardware role

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC architecture experience
No GPU / SIMD-MIMD / DNN optimization background
General software engineering breadth and craftsmanship
